body:
{
  "title": "Anchor All-Hands Q4 2027",
  "compiled_truth": "The Q4 2027 all-hands was held on January 8, 2028 at the usual Monday morning slot — full attendance for the first time in three months. [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) opened with a recap of the quarter, noting that [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) had closed its strongest revenue quarter to date, driven primarily by expansion contracts from existing customers and two net-new enterprise logos signed in November. Total ARR crossed a milestone that Seren described as \"not quite where the board wanted us, but close enough that nobody's losing sleep.\" She flagged that burn rate stayed flat despite headcount growing by four, which she credited to [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei)'s operational discipline on the finance side.\n\n[Miro Bergmann](people/miro-bergmann) presented the engineering retrospective. The single-pass primitives core had its first full quarter without a P0 incident, which drew genuine applause. Miro walked through the reliability metrics and noted that the deterministic pipeline's latency budgets were now consistently beating SLA targets by 15-20%. He also called out work from [Kael Petrov](people/kael-petrov) on the new edge deployment tooling, saying it was \"the kind of thing that doesn't show up in dashboards but saves us hours every week.\" Kael seemed uncomfortable with the praise but nodded.\n\n[Tal Kimura](people/tal-kimura) covered go-to-market. Pipeline for Q1 2028 looks healthy — 3x coverage on the quarterly target — though Tal cautioned that two of the larger deals have long procurement cycles and might slip. She highlighted a partnership conversation that [Paz Santos](people/paz-santos) had been leading with a logistics verticle player, which could open up a new segment for Anchor.\n\nOKRs for Q1 2028 were presented jointly by Seren and [Nova Mensah](people/nova-mensah). Key objectives: ship the v3.2 release with multi-tenant isolation improvements, close at least three new enterprise contracts, and finalize the Series B preparation materials. Nova emphasized that product-led growth metrics need more attention — trial-to-paid conversion dropped slightly in Q4 and she wants the team focused on understanding why.\n\n[Ari Leclerc](people/ari-leclerc) gave a brief update on the hiring pipeline. Two senior infrastructure engineers have accepted offers starting in February, and there's an open req for a developer relations lead. Ari mentioned the team is now at 27 people and growing carefully. The meeting wrapped with an open Q&A — most questions centered on Series B timing and whether the company would consider opening a second office. Seren deflected on the office question but confirmed that fundraising conversations are activley underway.",
  "timeline": [
    "- **2027-10-02** | Q4 kickoff planning session led by [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) and [Nova Mensah](people/nova-mensah) to set quarterly objectives",
    "- **2027-10-18** | First net-new enterprise logo of Q4 signed; deal sourced by [Tal Kimura](people/tal-kimura)",
    "- **2027-11-06** | [Kael Petrov](people/kael-petrov) ships edge deployment tooling internally, reducing release cycle overhead",
    "- **2027-11-15** | Second enterprise contract closed, bringing Q4 new logo count to two",
    "- **2027-11-29** | [Paz Santos](people/paz-santos) begins partnership exploration with logistics vertical prospect",
    "- **2027-12-11** | [Miro Bergmann](people/miro-bergmann) confirms full quarter with zero P0 incidents on the deterministic pipeline",
    "- **2027-12-19** | [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) circulates Q4 financial summary to leadership — burn rate held flat despite headcount growth",
    "- **2028-01-03** | [Ari Leclerc](people/ari-leclerc) confirms two senior infrastructure engineer acceptances for February start dates",
    "- **2028-01-08** | Q4 2027 all-hands held; full company attendance for the first time since October"
  ]
}
